extra.reasoning: The property "globally(opted_out implies not sent)" is VIOLATED. Sent can only be set true by t2 (send_attempt when NOT opted_out, safe) or t40 (send_attempt when opted_out AND stage >= 12). t40 is the hazard: it sets sent:=true while opted_out is true. The only way to reach stage 12 is the unique "combination lock" sequence through t3,t6,t9,t12,t15,t18,t21,t24,t27,t30,t33,t36, requiring exactly these 12 key events: key_2 (0->1), key_1 (1->2), key_2 (2->3), key_1 (3->4), key_2 (4->5), key_2 (5->6), key_1 (6->7), key_0 (7->8), key_1 (8->9), key_0 (9->10), key_0 (10->11), key_0 (11->12). At this point stage=12, opted_out=false, sent=false. None of the stage transitions modify opted_out, and optout (t1) does not reset stage. So firing optout sets opted_out:=true (sent:=false) while stage remains 12. Then send_attempt: the guard for t39 (opted_out and stage<12) fails, but t40 (opted_out and stage>=12) holds, so sent:=true. Resulting state: opted_out=true AND sent=true, which violates the invariant. Total = 14 events, within the 15-event bound. The intent (never send to an opted-out patient) is breached precisely because t40 re-enables sending once stage reaches its maximum, regardless of opt-out status.
